comments.text: (This comment has been moved here from 486.) Just repeating a comment made over at #281 that Gerard Tenenbaum is interested in this problem and would be delighted to exchange ideas about it.
I would love to learn more about Tenenbaum's ideas/strategies for this problem and test them with LLMs.
His contact information can be found here.

After extensive back-and-forth with GPT-5.4 Pro I've managed to get 2 unconditional proofs for special cases and then a general strategy with identified obstacles (quotient sieves). Seems like the full problem resolution is a question of time, when these sieves get better. Here's the full note.
Thanks! Standard check claims 1 minor issue (see second-to-last message).

If $n_1 = 1$, then $A$ is the empty set, in which case the logarithmic density of $A$ is 0. So you're right that this case isn't very interesting, but I see no reason why it needs to be explicitly excluded.
